page.title: Lyme disease — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Lyme disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Lyme disease: 127 alterations and 113 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Kundalini yoga for post-treatment lyme disease.
Lyme borreliosis patients exhibit a significant decrease of their serum carnitine concentrations.
Our study using multiple detection methods confirms that morgellons disease is a true somatic illness associated with borrelia spirochetes that causes lyme disease.
Pilot study of psilocybin in patients with post-treatment lyme disease.
Pycnogenol® intake for 6 months in patients with persistent symptoms of Lyme disease can help relieve the main symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
The effectiveness of microdosed psilocybin in the treatment of neuropsychiatric lyme disease.
Vaccination for rabies, varicella or Lyme disease may result in acute or subacute post-vaccination small fiber neuropathy.
Vitamin A and E levels are significantly lower in persons with anti-borrelia antibodies present.
Modulation of autophagy may be a potential target for anti-inflammatory therapy in patients with persistent Lyme disease.
Hyperglycemia impairs neutrophil responses to B. burgdorferi and clearance of these bacteria from multiple tissues.
Testosterone levels may have significance in determining rate and risk of infection in Lyme disease.
Vitamin A deficiency exacerbates Lyme-caused arthritis in mice.
Vitamin D prevent the progression to severe arthritis in mice exposed to Borrelia burgdorferi.
"Filament formation associated with spirochetal infection: a comparative approach to Morgellons disease."
A comprehensive review of herbal supplements used for persistent symptoms attributed to lyme disease.
Bee venom or melittin could be an effective antimicrobial agent for Lyme disease.
Cistus creticus extract has a growth inhibitory effect against Borrelia burgdorferi.
Evaluation of natural and botanical medicines for activity against growing and non-growing forms of B. burgdorferi.
Grapefruit Seed Extract is a Powerful in vitro Agent Against Motile and Cystic Forms of Borrelia burgdorferi sensu lato.
Identification of essential oils with strong activity against stationary phase Borrelia burgdorferi.
Manganese and manganese chelators may play an important role in the pathogenesis of Lyme disease.
Phytochemicals and micronutrients may enhance efficacy of doxycycline in combating all the morphological forms of studied Borrelia sp.
Selective essential oils from spice or culinary herbs have high activity against stationary phase and biofilm Borrelia burgdorferi.
Stevia leaf extract could be considered as an effective agent against B. burgdorferi.
There is lack of evidence identified through this high-quality search strategy on the efficacy of antibiotics for treatment of Lyme neuroborreliosis.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

clinical_trials.title: Effects of Psilocybin in Post-Treatment Lyme Disease

remission.disease: Lyme Disease / Post-Treatment Lyme Disease Syndrome (PTLDS)
remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Acute Lyme: spontaneous resolution in some untreated patients but dissemination and late manifestations develop; PTLDS (persistent symptoms after adequate antibiotics): affects 10-20% of patients; spontaneous resolution of PTLDS in 50-60% by 6 months
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Acute Lyme: 14-21 day doxycycline achieves 80-95% cure; PTLDS: no treatment shown superior to placebo in 4 large RCTs; prolonged antibiotic therapy (IDSA-condemned) not effective; symptom management only
remission.gap_size: Large for PTLDS — the field lacks an evidence-based treatment and has been mired in controversy over mechanism and management
remission.barrier_detail: PTLDS treatment is one of the most contentious areas in medicine; IDSA position (no benefit of prolonged antibiotics) vs ILADS position (chronic infection requiring extended treatment) — RCTs support IDSA; the patient experience of persistent symptoms is real but the mechanism may not be ongoing active infection; this creates a significant care gap for suffering patients
remission.last_soc_change: 2020 — IDSA/AAN/ACR joint guidelines updated (no significant change in management; prolonged antibiotics explicitly not recommended); ongoing NIH investment in PTLDS mechanism research post-COVID-19 analogy with Long COVID
remission.outdated_treatments: Prolonged oral/IV antibiotic therapy beyond standard course for PTLDS (4 large RCTs negative; significant antibiotic harm without benefit); hyperbaric oxygen therapy (no evidence); bismacine (injectable compound — FDA has issued warnings); samento/cat's claw without evidence
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Symptom-targeted management (sleep, pain, fatigue — same interventions as ME/CFS); cognitive rehabilitation for brain fog; immunomodulatory approaches (under investigation); doxycycline repurposing for anti-inflammatory (non-antibiotic) effects in PTLDS
remission.frontier_signal: mRNA Lyme vaccine (Pfizer/Valneva VLA15/PF-06882961 — Phase 3 VALOR trial 2023, 89% efficacy vs Lyme disease — major prevention breakthrough); host-directed anti-inflammatory therapy for PTLDS; PCR-based diagnostics improving sensitivity for delayed diagnosis
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
